One detail: "Maschinenschrift" (Typescript) is in brackets. This describes the format/medium. The rule asks for `format`: "Usually '8°', '8'', or '4°' - single value only". So "4°" is the correct value for format.

Usually titles don't end in periods unless it's an abbreviation. I'll strip the trailing period if it looks like sentence punctuation, but keep it if it's part of the title. In German bibliographic cards, the title often ends with a period. I will include the text as is, maybe trimming the final dot if it's clearly punctuation. "Beitrag zu den epileptischen Seelenstörungen" seems safer.

So I focus on the Dissertation data.

"s." usually means "siehe" (see).

This card describes the item itself.
So "Dissertation or thesis" is definitely correct.
